repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body:
Related to issue: https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/5751

Cleanup some of the simpler `distutils` usage cases with [their recommended replacements]( https://setuptools.pypa.io/en/latest/deprecated/distutils-legacy.html#prefer-setuptools ).

There is still much more work to do here, but at least this starts moving in the right direction incrementally.

comment_data.body: This alone fixes an issue with `%load_ext Cython`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -49,7 +49,7 @@
 import sys
 import time
 import copy
-import distutils.log
+import logging

comment_data.body: This was commented. So perhaps it could even be removed. However without knowing more about why it is here, just went with applying the recommended migration advice. Happy to defer to others judgment on this
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@
 import tempfile
 import subprocess
 #import distutils.core
-#from distutils import sysconfig
+#import sysconfig

comment_data.body:
Arguably this environment variable could be renamed. Held off doing that for now, but can add if that is preferred

Edit: Should add `sysconfig` is a builtin. So the `ImportError` shouldn't be needed any more. Can clean this up as well
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -33,7 +33,7 @@
 
 
 try:
-    from distutils import sysconfig
+    import sysconfig
     DISTUTILS_CFLAGS = sysconfig.get_config_var('CFLAGS')
 except ImportError:
     DISTUTILS_CFLAGS = ''

comment_data.body:
I don't totally follow what this function is doing. The comment indicates there are issues with it.

Have stuck to making the simple `sysconfig` change. However `ImportError` shouldn't occur in this case as `sysconfig` is a builtin. So a little unsure on what this should be ok.

Happy to make a recommended change.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -230,7 +230,7 @@ def exists(env):
     try:
         # This is not quite right: if someone defines all variables by himself,
         # it would work without distutils
-        from distutils import sysconfig
+        import sysconfig
         return True
     except ImportError:
         return False

comment_data.body:
It looks like `sysconfig.set_python_build()` is not defined

```python
In [1]: import sysconfig
   ...: 
   ...: sysconfig.set_python_build()
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[1], line 3
      1 import sysconfig
----> 3 sysconfig.set_python_build()

AttributeError: module 'sysconfig' has no attribute 'set_python_build'
```

Will roll this back

```suggestion
if 1:
    from distutils import sysconfig
    try:
        sysconfig.set_python_build()
    except AttributeError:
        pass
    import pyxbuild
    print(pyxbuild.distutils.sysconfig == sysconfig)
```
Done in later changes in this PR

I think this needs to be

```suggestion
import logging
import distutils
```

based on the failure of coverage tests
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -49,7 +49,7 @@
 import sys
 import time
 import copy
-import distutils.log
+import logging

comment_data.body: I think this is `setlevel` rather than `set_threshold` in the logging module

I'm not quite sure of the status of `old_build_ext` and whether it's used any more.

I can see a couple of uses of `log` in the file though
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -8,13 +8,14 @@
 
 __revision__ = "$Id:$"
 
-import sys
+import logging
 import os
+import sys
+import sysconfig
+
 from distutils.errors import DistutilsPlatformError
 from distutils.dep_util import newer, newer_group
-from distutils import log

commit.message:
Replace `distutils.log` with `logging`

As `distutils` is dropped starting with Python 3.12, move from using
`distutils.log` to its recommended replacement the builtin `logging`
module.

commit.message:
Replace `distutils.sysconfig` with `sysconfig`

As `distutils` is dropped starting with Python 3.12, move from using
`distutils.sysconfig` to its recommended replacement the builtin
`sysconfig` module.

commit.message:
Revert to `distutils.sysconfig` for `set_python_build()`

As `sysconfig` does not define `set_python_build()`, rollback to using
`distutils.sysconfig` in this instance.

This will eventually need to be addressed somehow to handle Python
3.12+'s removal of `distutils`. It just may involve more significant
changes.

committer jakirkham <jakirkham@gmail.com> 1780100795 -0700

Revert to `distutils.sysconfig` for `set_python_build()`

As `sysconfig` does not define `set_python_build()`, rollback to using
`distutils.sysconfig` in this instance.

This will eventually need to be addressed somehow to handle Python
3.12+'s removal of `distutils`. It just may involve more significant
changes.

title: Apply some simpler `distutils` migration advice
